name: Graphical User Interface
description: Build reactive UIs with controls, layout areas, and data binding

content.title: Graphical User Interface
content.content:
# Graphical User Interface

MeshWeaver provides a control-based GUI system that renders reactive user interfaces from C# code.

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Purpose |
|---------|----------|
| **Controls** | Declarative UI elements that define what to render |
| **Layout Areas** | Named regions that display controls |
| **Data Binding** | Automatic synchronization between UI and data |
| **Skins** | Styling and appearance customization |

## How It Works

You create controls using the `Controls` class:

```csharp
var button = Controls.Button("Click Me");
var label = Controls.Label("Hello World");
var stack = Controls.Stack
    .WithView(label)
    .WithView(button);
```

Controls are immutable records. Each `With*` method returns a new control instance with the specified modification.

## Control Categories

**Container Controls**: Organize other controls
- Stack, Tabs, Splitter, Layout, LayoutGrid

**Input Controls**: Accept user data
- TextField, NumberField, CheckBox, Select, ComboBox, DatePicker

**Display Controls**: Show information
- Label, Badge, Icon, Markdown, Html, CodeSample

**Data Controls**: Show collections
- DataGrid, Editor

**Action Controls**: Trigger operations
- Button, MenuItem, NavLink

## Documentation

### Concepts

Core patterns and principles for building reactive UIs:
- WithView patterns for adding controls to containers
- Static vs dynamic views and when they update
- Data binding and data flow
- Property attributes for forms and validation

[Explore Concepts](MeshWeaver/GUI/Concepts)

---

### Controls Reference

Detailed documentation for available UI controls:
- Editor - Generate forms from C# records
- Stack - Vertical and horizontal layouts
- DataGrid - Tabular data display

[Explore Controls](MeshWeaver/GUI/Controls)

## Quick Example

```csharp
public record Person
{
    [Required]
    public string Name { get; init; }
    public int Age { get; init; }
}

// Create an editable form with reactive result
Mesh.Edit(
    new Person(),
    p => Controls.Label($"Hello {p.Name}, age {p.Age}")
)
```

Result: A vertical layout containing an auto-generated form for Person and a label that updates as you type.
